# DEVOCEAN Lodge — Full Content

> Small, family-run eco-lodge in Ponta do Ouro, far-southern Mozambique — 20 minutes from the South African border at Kosi Bay. Safari tents, a garden cottage, and a thatched chalet a short walk from the beach. Hosted by Sean. Known for world-class scuba diving, ethical dolphin swims, whale watching, and game safaris. Not a resort — warm, authentic, personal.

DEVOCEAN Lodge is operated by TERRAfrique LDA in Ponta do Ouro (Matutuine, Maputo Province, Mozambique), at coordinates -26.8420, 32.8850. Rates are billed in US dollars. Check-in from 14:00, check-out by 10:00. Free Wi-Fi. Pets not allowed.

---

## Accommodation

DEVOCEAN Lodge offers four accommodation types, each with a private terrace in a lush tropical garden setting a short walk from the beach. All units include free Wi-Fi.

### Safari Tent
12 m² canvas tent on a raised 3×6 m platform. Sleeps 2.

Amenities:
- Twin beds (pushed together as King on request) with pedestals and shaded lamps
- Mosquito mesh on all doors and windows
- Strong fan and power points
- Private terrace with rolled palm-leaf chairs facing the tropical garden
- Shared ablutions (ladies / gents): 2 hot-and-cold showers and 2 toilets each

Page: https://devoceanlodge.com/safari.html

### Comfort Tent
Upgraded 12 m² canvas tent with added privacy (solid side and back walls), en-suite bathroom, and private terrace. Sleeps 2.

Amenities:
- Twin/King bed setup, lamps, convenient light switches
- Mosquito mesh, strong fan, power points
- Private wood terrace with rolled palm-leaf chairs
- Private bathroom (shower, toilet, sink) under a grass-thatched roof at the rear

Page: https://devoceanlodge.com/comfort.html

### Garden Cottage
Airy standalone cottage with queen bed, inverter air-conditioning (cooling and heating), and a charming private bathroom in a thatched roundavel. Sleeps 2.

Amenities:
- Queen bed with working desk and shaded lamp
- Dining table with chairs
- Inverter air-conditioning (reliable power, no generator noise)
- Private bathroom in a grass-thatched roundavel (shower, sink, toilet)
- Private wooden terrace with camping chairs and side table

Page: https://devoceanlodge.com/cottage.html

### Thatched Chalet
Secluded, romantic chalet under palms and strelitzia. The most private unit at the lodge. Sleeps 2.

Amenities:
- Twin beds or King (on request) with pedestals
- Dinner table with chairs
- Air-conditioning
- Private bathroom (shower, sink, toilet)
- Private terrace with palm-leaf chairs

Page: https://devoceanlodge.com/chalet.html

---

## Experiences & Activities

### Scuba Diving
Ponta do Ouro offers world-class scuba diving with 20+ dive sites ranging from shallow reefs to deep pinnacles. The crystal-clear waters of the Mozambique Channel provide exceptional visibility and encounters with 19 species of sharks.

Pricing: From US$40 per dive | Two-dive package: US$75–90 | PADI Open Water course: US$350–400. Equipment rental, boat transport, dive master, and marine park fees included.

Duration: Morning trips (2 dives, 3–4 hours) or full-day (3 dives, 6–8 hours). Beach surf launch — expect to get wet.

Skill level: Beginner to Advanced. Minimum age 10 (Junior Open Water). PADI certification required, or enrol in an Open Water course on site.

Top dive sites:
- Pinnacles (28–40 m) — Premier shark dive: bull sharks, tiger sharks, hammerheads, mantas
- Bass City (20–25 m) — Potato bass, lionfish, zebra sharks, cleaning stations
- Wayne's World (~20 m) — Macro paradise: seahorses, frogfish, ghost pipefish
- Atlantis (35–47 m) — Deep dive: eagle rays, reef sharks, Spanish dancers
- Doodles (14–18 m) — All levels: rays, turtles, moray eels, octopus
- Beginner sites: Crèche, Playground (10–18 m)
- Advanced: Cloudbreak (28–48 m)

Seasonal highlights:
- Bull and tiger sharks: November–April (water 26–29°C)
- Hammerheads: April–October (best visibility)
- Whale sharks: October–March
- Humpback whales: July–November
- Best visibility: May–August (20–30 m)
- Warmest water: December–February (27–29°C)

Operators: Back to Basics Adventures (backtobasicsadventures@gmail.com), Gozo Azul Diving (natalie@gozo-azul.co.za, PADI 5-star), Under Water Explorer (herb@thewhaler.co.za), Blowing Bubbles Diving (info@blowingbubbles.co.za), Shark Diving Mozambique (info@sharkdivingmozambique.com), Oceana Dive Center (oceanamozdiving@gmail.com).

Page: https://devoceanlodge.com/experiences/diving

### Dolphin Swimming
Ethical wild Indo-Pacific bottlenose dolphin encounters inside the Ponta do Ouro Partial Marine Reserve. Operators follow strict passive-interaction guidelines — no chasing, no touching, dolphins choose the encounter.

200+ resident dolphins live year-round in the marine reserve. Trips depart by boat to meet them in their natural habitat. Swimming ability required.

Ethical practices operators follow:
- Passive interaction only — dolphins approach on their own terms
- No feeding or touching
- Boats maintain respectful distance until dolphins choose to engage
- Research and photo-ID data collected to support conservation

Operators: Dolphin Encountours (https://www.dolphinencountours.org/), The Dolphin Centre (https://thedolphincentre.com/)

Page: https://devoceanlodge.com/experiences/dolphins

### Ocean Seafaris (Whale Watching)
Boat-based ocean safaris for humpback whale watching (July–November), year-round dolphin encounters, seasonal whale shark sightings, and snorkelling over pristine reefs.

30,000 humpback whales migrate through Mozambican waters each year. Whale behaviours commonly seen: breaching (full body launch), tail-slapping, spy-hopping, flipper slapping. Whales often approach boats.

Pricing: Whale watching only: US$40 | Combined dolphin + whale: US$45 | Full-day private tour from Maputo: US$179. Children under 12: discounted rates. Equipment and snacks included.

Duration: 2–3 hours (half-day); 6–8 hours (full-day from Maputo). Morning departures for best conditions.

All ages. No special skills for whale watching; swimming ability needed for snorkelling. Warm jacket recommended July–September.

Seasonal calendar:
- Humpback whales: July–November (peak July–October)
- Dolphins: Year-round (200+ residents)
- Whale sharks: October–March
- Manta rays: December–May
- Sea turtles: October–March (nesting season)
- Calmest seas: June–September (winter)

Operators: The Dolphin Centre (https://thedolphincentre.com/), Dolphin Encountours (https://www.dolphinencountours.org/), Gozo Azul (https://gozo-azul.co.za/)

Page: https://devoceanlodge.com/experiences/seafari

### Game Safaris (Maputo National Park)
Maputo National Park (formerly Maputo Special Reserve, UNESCO World Heritage Site 2025) is approximately 45 minutes from Ponta do Ouro — 1,718 km² of protected coastal wilderness.

Wildlife: 450–500 coastal elephants (year-round, main attraction), giraffes, zebras, hippos and crocodiles around lakes and wetlands, reedbuck, impala, nyala, eland, kudu, waterbuck, red duiker. Predators: leopard, cheetah, spotted hyena (reintroduced 2023–24). 300+ bird species including flamingos.

Pricing: Day safari US$95–120 per person | Full-day with Ponta do Ouro beach US$100–130 | 2–3 day camping safari from US$200. 4x4 vehicle, professional guide, park fees, binoculars, water and snacks included.

Duration: Depart Ponta do Ouro early morning (6–7 AM), return late afternoon (4–5 PM). Half-day: 3–4 hours in park.

All ages and fitness levels. Vehicle-based viewing — no walking required. 4x4 mandatory (thick sand tracks inside park). Neutral clothing (khaki, beige, olive) recommended.

Best time: July–October (dry season — animals concentrate at water sources; cooler 18–25°C). Bird watching peak October–March. Avoid December–February (hot, humid, dense vegetation).

Operators: Maputo National Park (reservas@parquemaputo.gov.mz), Transportes Mulungo (transportesmulungo@gmail.com).

Official park brochure (PDF, 7.3 MB): https://devoceanlodge.com/downloads/MaputoNationalPark_Brochure.pdf

Page: https://devoceanlodge.com/experiences/safari

### Beach & Deep-Sea Fishing
Mozambique's southern waters are renowned for big-game fishing. Offshore: marlin, sailfish, wahoo, yellowfin tuna, king mackerel. From the beach: kingfish, barracuda, pompano.

Day and half-day boat charters available. All skill levels.

Operator: Mozambique Fishin' Charters (https://mozambiquefishincharters.co.za/packages/)

Page: https://devoceanlodge.com/experiences/fishing

### Surfing
Ponta do Ouro is home to a classic right-hand point break with consistent waves. Rides of 100–200 m, up to 1 km in ideal conditions. Surfboards and lessons available for beginners and intermediates.

Operator: SPIGS Surf's Up (https://www.instagram.com/spigssurfsup/)

Page: https://devoceanlodge.com/experiences/surfing

### Lighthouse Walk
Hike to the old lighthouse on the headland for sweeping Indian Ocean panoramas. Dolphins and turtles commonly spotted from the top. An easy, rewarding walk from the village — no guide required.

Map: https://maps.app.goo.gl/frVsczs798Ym1wDe9

Page: https://devoceanlodge.com/experiences/lighthouse

---

## Location & Getting There

**Address**: Ponta do Ouro, Matutuine, Maputo Province, Mozambique. Coordinates: -26.8420, 32.8850.
Map: https://maps.app.goo.gl/edhq5PGLLhGMh9rL6

**From South Africa (by road)**
The Kosi Bay / Ponta do Ouro border crossing (KwaZulu-Natal) is open daily 08:00–17:00, approximately 20 minutes from the lodge. The road from the border into Ponta do Ouro village is fully tarmacked — no 4x4 required to reach the lodge. A 4x4 is only needed for outlying areas (Ponta Malongane, Ponta Mamoli, Santa Maria).

Fuel: available in Manguzi (South Africa, before the border) and in Ponta do Ouro village.

**From Maputo**
Ponta do Ouro is approximately 120 km south of Maputo. Private transfers, shuttle services, and self-drive are all options.

---

## Booking & Rates

Book online with real-time availability check and Stripe deposit payment: https://devoceanlodge.com/book-direct

**Deposit policy**: 50% deposit at time of booking; balance paid on arrival. Last-minute bookings (within the cancellation window) require 100% payment upfront.

**Currency**: All rates in US dollars (USD). Payment accepted in major currencies.

**Policies**:
- Check-in: from 14:00
- Check-out: by 10:00
- Free Wi-Fi throughout the property
- Pets not allowed
- The reservations team personally confirms every booking

**Contact**:
- Email: info@devoceanlodge.com
- Phone / WhatsApp: +258 84 418 2252

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Do I need a 4x4 to get to the lodge?**
No. The road from the Kosi Bay border to Ponta do Ouro village is fully tarmacked. A 4x4 is only required if you plan to visit outlying areas such as Ponta Malongane, Ponta Mamoli, or Santa Maria.

**What is the best time of year to visit?**
Year-round — each season has different highlights. July–November for humpback whales. October–March for whale sharks. November–April for bull and tiger sharks and warmest water. July–October for game safaris (dry season). May–August for best diving visibility.

**Is scuba diving certification required?**
Yes — a PADI or equivalent certification is required to dive. You can complete a PADI Open Water course on site (US$350–400) if you are not yet certified. Minimum age is 10 years (Junior Open Water).

**Are the dolphin encounters ethical?**
Yes. All operators in the Ponta do Ouro Partial Marine Reserve follow strict passive-interaction guidelines. Dolphins are never chased or touched; they approach on their own terms. Trips are research-linked — photo-ID data is collected to support ongoing conservation work.

**Is Maputo National Park safe?**
Yes — game drives are conducted in enclosed 4x4 vehicles with professional guides who know the park. The park is a UNESCO World Heritage Site with active conservation management.

**What currency should I bring?**
US dollars are the easiest. South African rand is widely accepted near the border. Mozambican metical (MZN) is the local currency but less convenient for tourist services. Lodge rates are billed in USD.

**Is there reliable power at the lodge?**
The Garden Cottage and Thatched Chalet have inverter air-conditioning (reliable, quiet). All units have power points. The lodge has backup power arrangements for guest comfort.

**Can I book multiple activities during my stay?**
Absolutely. The lodge team will connect you with the best local operators for your chosen activities. Many guests combine diving or whale watching in the morning with beach time, a lighthouse walk, or an afternoon in the garden in the evenings.
